body:
Add frontend unit tests:
Data Adapters:
- adaptServerCard: Card data sent from the backend can be correctly converted to the frontend format.
- adaptPveGameState: Battle state data from the backend can be correctly converted to the frontend Socket cache format.
- deriveGameOver: Battle settlement data from the backend can correctly trigger the "win/loss pop-up" state on the frontend.

Attack Attribute Derivation:
- null input: Passing in null is considered a normal attack.
- empty array: Passing in an empty array is considered a normal attack.
- undefined: Passing in undefined is considered a normal attack.
- single red card: A single red card is considered a fire attribute.
- single blue card: A single blue card is considered a water attribute.
- single green card: A single green card is considered a grass attribute.
- all red cards: A combination of all red cards is considered a fire attribute.
- all blue cards: A combination of all blue cards is considered a water attribute.
- all green cards: A combination of all green cards is considered a grass attribute.
- mixed red and blue: A combination of red and blue cards is considered a normal attack.
- mixed red and green: A mix of red and green cards is considered a normal attack.
- unknown color: Cards of unknown color are considered normal attacks.
- null card in array: When the deck contains empty cards, it's considered a normal attack.
- 5-Card All-Fire Hand: A hand full of 5 red cards is considered a fire card.

Card Selection Logic:
- select new card adds it: Clicking an unselected card adds it to the selected list.
- deselect removes it: Clicking a selected card removes it from the list.
- cannot exceed MAX_SELECT=5: After selecting 5 cards, no new cards can be selected.
- can deselect when at MAX_SELECT: When selecting 5 cards, deselecting existing cards is still allowed.
- select then deselect same card: Selecting and then deselecting the same card emptys the list.

Rogue Buff Pool:
- SKILL_ENERGY_MAX is one-time: Verifies that "Max Energy" is a one-time relic.
- ELEMENT_CHIP_MULT is stackable: Verifies that "Elemental Multiplier" is a stackable relic.
- Owned one-time buff excluded: Owned one-time relics will be removed from the drop pool.
- Owned stackable buff NOT excluded: Owned stackable relics will not be removed from the pool.
- No owned buffs -> full pool: The pool is full when there are no relics.
- Multiple one-time owned -> all excluded: Multiple owned one-time relics will be correctly removed from the pool.

Save File Restoration and Reset:
- buildRestorePayload: extracts enhancements...: Correctly extracts buff data from save points and sends it to the backend for reconnection after disconnection.
- buildRestorePayload: empty/null enhancements: Empty save files are handled correctly without errors.
- buildRestorePayload: buffs extracted correctly...: Accurately extracts core buff data from the save file.
- buildInitialRogueState: When resetting the game, all rogue states revert to their default values.
- restartGame: After restarting the game, relics and tower floors are completely cleared.

Rogue Enhancements:
- first enhancement added: The first relic acquired is correctly added to the inventory.
- second enhancement stacks: Stackable relics of the same type are correctly added to the inventory.
- different enhancement types stack independently: Different types of relics are stored independently.
- three different enhancements accumulate: The total number of three different relics acquired consecutively is correctly added together.
- buffs extracted correctly from enhancements: The pure buff field is accurately extracted from the entire relic object.
- enhancements without buff field are filtered out: Placeholder relics without actual buffs are not sent to the settlement system.
- Stackable buff (HP_BONUS) appears...: Stackable attributes (such as HP) will be correctly extracted.
- Stacking same buff type keeps both entries: When stacking the same buff, the system will retain two active entries.

Rogue Transitions and Timers:
- showEnhancementAfterAnimation: null pendingLayer: Intercepts the pop-up when there are no pending layers.
- showEnhancementAfterAnimation: clears pendingLayer: Clears the marker after the pop-up is triggered to prevent repeated pop-ups of the buff selection interface.
- battleWin: sets victoryTriggered: Marks a victory and intercepts duplicate victory settlements (prevents replaying).
- battleLose: layer routing logic: Tests the failure logic (death on layer 1 ends the game directly; subsequent deaths on subsequent layers allow retrying the current layer).
- victoryTriggered reset after confirmEnhancement: Resets the victory marker after selecting relics, allowing the next layer to correctly determine the winner.
- The auto-save timer is cancelled on cleanup: This ensures the auto-save timer is properly destroyed upon exiting the game, preventing memory leaks.
- Auto-save fires if not cancelled: During normal gameplay, auto-save will execute as scheduled.

All frontend tests passed
title: feat: frontend unit test
